document_title: Orient Global - Comprehensive UI/UX Restructuring & CMS Architecture Master Plan
author: Elite Web Solutions Expert (Lead UI/UX, Fullstack, Systems Architect)
executive_summary: This document outlines a complete overhaul of the Orient Global web platform. It bridges a highly tailored, division-specific frontend aesthetic with a robust, centralized Fullstack CMS architecture. The goal is to transform the platform into a living, breathing digital ecosystem where each division (Bakery, Market, Dining, Games, Water, Lounge) possesses a unique layout density and design language, all powered by a seamless, weekly-updatable backend infrastructure.

core_philosophy.vision: Intentional Pairings over Defaults. The UI will shift from a monolithic template to a multi-archetype system.
core_philosophy.white_space_strategy: Macro white space (between sections) will remain consistent to unify the brand, while micro white space (between elements) will scale dynamically based on the division's archetype.
core_philosophy.typography_system: A shared sans-serif (Inter) for utility, paired with division-specific display fonts (e.g., Playfair Display for Dining, JetBrains Mono for Games).

page_archetypes.density: High Density - Tightly packed, interactive, data-rich.
layout_strategy: Grid-heavy layouts with visible borders (1px solid neon/accent colors).
Monospace typography for leaderboards, hardware specs, and live tournament brackets.
Hover-invert interactions and marquee animations for live updates.
Minimal padding (e.g., 0.5rem to 1rem) to maximize screen real estate for game catalogs and active player counts.

page_archetypes.density: Low Density - Highly breathable, airy, expansive.
layout_strategy: Massive use of negative space. Elements should feel like they are floating.
Large, light-weight typography (e.g., 300 weight) to convey purity and clarity.
Soft, layered radial gradients and glassmorphism (backdrop-filter blur) to simulate water and transparency.
Generous padding (e.g., 4rem to 8rem) between sections to force a slow, calming scroll experience.
page_archetypes.color_palette: Pure whites, aero-whites, soft sky-blues, transparent overlays.

layout_strategy: Split 50/50 layouts contrasting deep blacks with high-resolution, rich food photography.
Oversized, elegant serif typography (e.g., Cormorant Garamond) for menu categories and chef quotes.
Thin horizontal divider lines (1px rgba(255,255,255,0.2)) to separate menu items without visual clutter.
Subtle parallax effects on imagery to create depth.
page_archetypes.color_palette: Noir black, warm gold/primary accents, muted secondary text.

layout_strategy: Pill-shaped imagery and large rounded corners (32px) to evoke softness and dough.
Warm off-white backgrounds to reduce eye strain and feel organic.
Card-based layouts with soft drop shadows for product displays.
Classic serif fonts mixed with clean sans-serif for product descriptions.
page_archetypes.color_palette: Warm off-whites, crust-browns, soft primary orange.

layout_strategy: Strict bento-box or masonry grid layouts for product categories and daily deals.
System fonts for maximum legibility.
Clear visual hierarchy using font weights (bold prices, muted descriptions).
Sticky filtering sidebars and dense product cards to allow quick scanning of inventory.
page_archetypes.color_palette: Crisp whites, light grays, high-contrast black text.

layout_strategy: Full-bleed background videos or deeply blurred imagery.
Grid navigation with thin borders, feeling systematic yet high-end.
Vertical text elements (writing-mode: vertical-rl) for graphic sophistication.
Oval-masked images for VIP sections and signature cocktails.
page_archetypes.color_palette: Deep amber, velvet reds, pitch black.

objective_2_cms_architecture.system_overview: A decoupled architecture where the React/Vite frontend consumes a RESTful/GraphQL API served by a Node.js/Express backend. The Admin Dashboard acts as the central nervous system, pushing state to a managed database (e.g., PostgreSQL for structured data, MongoDB for flexible content blocks).

database_architecture.core_concept: Entity-Attribute-Value (EAV) mixed with JSONB columns to allow flexible, division-specific schemas without database migrations every week.

data_flow_and_crud.create: Admin uploads new menu items or hero images via the Dashboard. Media is piped directly to an S3 bucket/Cloudinary. The returned URL is saved in the `ContentBlocks` payload.
data_flow_and_crud.read: Frontend components use React Query (or SWR) to fetch `ContentBlocks` by `division_id`. A Redis caching layer ensures high-speed delivery of the live site, invalidating only when a new weekly update is published.
data_flow_and_crud.update: Admins edit JSON payloads via a visual block editor in the CMS. Changes are saved as a 'Draft' in `WeeklyUpdates`.
data_flow_and_crud.delete: Soft-delete implementation. Records are marked `is_deleted: true` to maintain historical analytics and allow rollbacks.

public_api: GET /api/v1/content/:division_slug - Fetches all active content blocks for a division.
admin_api_protected: POST /api/admin/media/upload - Multipart form upload to CDN.
POST /api/admin/publish - Triggers cache invalidation and moves drafts to live.

weekly_update_workflow.step_1_drafting: Division managers log into the CMS and modify their specific sections (e.g., Bakery manager updates the 'Pastry of the Week').
weekly_update_workflow.step_2_preview: The CMS generates a live, sandboxed preview URL using the exact frontend components, injecting the draft JSON payload.
weekly_update_workflow.step_3_approval: The 'admin_boss' reviews the aggregated weekly changes in the 'Authorizations' inbox.
weekly_update_workflow.step_4_deployment: Upon approval, the `status` flips to `published`. The Node server flushes the Redis cache for affected routes, and the frontend instantly reflects the new data via WebSockets or the next client-side fetch.

implementation_phases.focus: Set up PostgreSQL/MongoDB schemas, configure S3 for media, and build the core Express API routes.
Upgrade the current dashboard to include a visual JSON block editor, media library, and the Draft/Publish workflow.
Refactor the existing frontend. Implement the 6 distinct page archetypes using Tailwind CSS, ensuring strict adherence to the density and white-space rules.
Connect the new frontend to the live API. Implement React Query for state management and Redis for sub-10ms response times.
